channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: [AINews] Thinky's Inkling: 975B-A41B multimodal, new best American Apache 2.0 open model (with Inkling-Small, 276B-A12B)
item.description: Thinky's first full LLM release is a banger and bonus: it's open weights!

item.title: Trend Micro, Tanium, ESET and Tenable Patch Severe Product Vulnerabilities
item.description: The cybersecurity companies patched critical and high-severity vulnerabilities in some of their products. The post Trend Micro, Tanium, ESET and Tenable Patch Severe Product Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: UPDATE: Google Cloud VMware Engine (GCVE), Google Cloud NetApp Volumes, and Bare Metal Solutions (BMS) services are experiencing a service outage in europe-west4-a due to a cooling failure.
item.description: Incident began at 2026-07-15 16:57 (all times are US/Pacific).Summary Google Cloud VMware Engine (GCVE), Google Cloud NetApp Volumes, and Bare Metal Solutions (BMS) services are experiencing a service outage in europe-west4-a due to a cooling failure Description The datacenter serving...

item.title: OriginBlame: Record- and Token-Level Data Provenance for AI Training Datasets
item.description: arXiv:2607.13037v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: When a data contributor requests removal, model trainers face a practical gap: unlearning algorithms require a forget set, yet no tool can locate which training records belong to a given author. Existing provenance systems operate at file or dataset...

item.title: SPINE: Bridging the Cyber-Physical Gap with Agentic AI
item.description: arXiv:2607.13049v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Foundation models have given robots a sophisticated brain for complex decision-making, yet deploying that intelligence into a physical platform still demands tedious, expert-driven calibration. This deployment gap, the robot's spinal cord, remains a...

item.title: Interventional Grounding Audits: Black-Box Premise-Dependency Tests for LLM Chain-of-Thought via Predicate Substitution
item.description: arXiv:2607.13069v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models produce chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning that appears logically sound yet may not genuinely depend on its stated premises. We introduce interventional grounding audits, a black-box, step-level test of premise dependency: we...

item.title: Probabilistic Extension of Neuro-Symbolic AGI Robots based on Belnap's Typed Intensional FOL
item.description: arXiv:2607.13073v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Neuro-symbolic AI based on $IFOL_B$ is a way to combine neural learning and symbolic reasoning to overcome limitations of purely neural systems (like lack of interpretability and logical structure) with formal logical machinery for self-reference. In...

item.title: Self-Improvements in Modern Agentic Systems: A Survey
item.description: arXiv:2607.13104v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Self-improving autonomous agents are moving from research prototypes to deployed systems. The primary goal is controllable evolution, or adaptation, from experience with minimal or even no human input. This survey frames modern self-improving agents...

item.title: Automatic Differentiation from Scratch: How PyTorch Computes Gradients in Physics-Informed Neural Networks
item.description: arXiv:2607.13042v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper traces, with explicit numerical values, how PyTorch's automatic differentiation (AD) engine computes gradients for Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) training -- a setting that requires two levels of differentiation: computing the...

item.title: Beyond Backbone Backpropagation: A Decoupled Strategy for Efficient Transfer Learning
item.description: arXiv:2607.13043v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep learning models achieve state-of-the-art image classification but face deployment challenges due to computational costs and energy demands. We propose a lightweight training strategy that adapts normalization layers of the model to the new domain...

item.title: Federated Explainable Artificial Intelligence: Roles, Architectures, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
item.description: arXiv:2607.13045v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Federated Learning (FL) has emerged as a key paradigm for privacy-preserving collaborative model training across distributed and heterogeneous data sources. By keeping raw data local, FL addresses data confidentiality concerns, yet it does not resolve...

item.title: What Your Model Threw Away and Why You'll Want It Back: Masking, Fingerprinting, and Privacy from Discarded Geometry
item.description: arXiv:2607.13046v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We develop a framework for the information discarded by machine learning models whose inputs carry a Lie group action. Given a representation $\pi$ of a Lie group $G$ on a space $V$ and a learned function $f\colon V \to \mathbb{R}$, we define two...

item.title: Targeted Recovery of Weight-Space Mechanisms From Neural Networks
item.description: arXiv:2607.13047v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Parameter decomposition (PD) decomposes neural networks into interpretable computational components that faithfully reflect the original network's operations. However, scaling PD to large models requires vast compute, making it a costly and risky...

item.title: FixItFlow: Automated Troubleshooting Guide Generation from Cloud Incidents
item.description: arXiv:2607.13035v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Cloud services experience frequent incidents that require rapid diagnosis and resolution. Troubleshooting guides help engineers respond consistently, but creating them manually is labor-intensive, resulting in incomplete coverage and outdated...

item.title: Ask Before You Diagnose: Safe-Psych, a Sequential Evaluation Benchmark for LLMs in Psychiatry
item.description: arXiv:2607.13036v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for decision support in healthcare, but clinical evidence is often incomplete or evolving. When the available information is insufficient to support a reliable answer, models should request...

item.title: The Perplexity Trap: When Patent Law Makes Human Writing Look Like AI
item.description: arXiv:2607.13044v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The European Patent Office (EPO) reported record filings in 2025, and the 2026 EPO Guidelines hold applicants strictly responsible for LLM-assisted content under Article 83 and Rule 42, creating pressure to triage suspected AI-generated patent text....

item.title: Do LLMs Need Architectural Changes for Simultaneous Speech Translation? A Prefix-to-Prefix Data Driven Approach
item.description: arXiv:2607.13158v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Simultaneous speech translation (SimulST) requires incremental translation under strict latency constraints, yet remains challenging for decoder-only LLM systems due to limited context and cross-lingual reordering. Recent approaches often introduce...

item.title: What Models Express, Suppress, and Resist: Auditing Open-Weight LLMs with Persona Vectors
item.description: arXiv:2607.13162v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: What a language model will and will not do is largely set during post-training, but which behaviors it expresses, hides, or resists is not revealed by prompting alone. Persona vectors, behavioral directions in activation space, can probe this...

item.title: Neuracle Writes First Brain-Computer Interface Prescription: World First Invasive BCI Product Enters Clinical Use
item.description: Neuracle NEO-ONE SCI receives first prescription at Shanghai Huashan Hospital, completing the world first full commercial cycle of an invasive BCI device from approval to surgery to insurance coverage.

item.title: Seven Phone Models Receive China AI Filing as Apple, Xiaomi, OPPO and Others Confirm On-Device LLM Compliance
item.description: China cyberspace authorities approve seven on-device generative AI services including Apple Intelligence, with Alibaba Qwen, Xiaomi MiMo, and OPPO Andes among registered models.

item.title: China First Full-Size Humanoid Autonomously Completes Long-Horizon Household Tasks Behind a Counter-Consensus Brain System
item.description: LimX Dynamics Oli humanoid performs end-to-end household chores with COSA 0.5 brain system, achieving global second Figure-level capability with a three-tier S2-S1-S0 architecture.

item.title: Alibaba Bets $10.6B on CXMT: The Strategy Behind China Largest Memory Maker IPO Support
item.description: Alibaba invests 7.6 billion RMB in CXMT ChangXin Memory Technologies ahead of its 29.5B RMB IPO, becoming the largest industrial investor with $130B estimated equity value post-listing.

item.title: Ant Group AI Strategy Tightens: Lingguang Retreats as A-Fu Takes Center Stage
item.description: Ant Group restructures AI strategy as Lingguang general model pivots to exploration while health-focused A-Fu becomes main strategic thrust, with 28.97M MAU targeting medical vertical.

item.title: Cloudflare R2 Issues
item.description: Jul 16, 01:12 UTC Identified - Cloudflare is investigating elevated error rates affecting R2 and some services that depend on it, including Stream, Pipelines, and Log Explorer. Customers may have experienced increased errors reading or writing objects and delayed data processing. Impact was...

item.title: TuxBot v3 Evolution Shows Signs of LLM-Assisted IoT Botnet Development
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a previously unreported Internet-of-Things (IoT) botnet framework dubbed TuxBot v3 Evolution that shows signs of being developed with assistance from a large language model (LLM), albeit with not so successful results. "While the AI complied with...

item.title: What building Shippy taught us about building agents
Model Routing Is Simple. Until It Isn’t.
Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer
item.description: OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other models boost their defenses against cyberattacks. Last week the company released the latest version of its flagship LLM, GPT-5.6. OpenAI says that training it against GPT-Red made the model its...

item.title: Network Performance Issues in AMS
item.description: Jul 15, 15:49 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 15, 14:53 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 15, 14:19 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 15, 13:39 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is...

item.title: Elevated errors in ENAM region
item.description: Jul 15, 15:45 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 15, 15:28 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 15, 15:22 UTC Investigating - Customers may experience elevated error rates in ENAM region

item.title: OkoBot Malware Framework Injects Seed Phrase Phishing Into Ledger and Trezor Apps
item.description: A malware framework called OkoBot has been running on Windows machines since April 2025, and one of its modules is built to con hardware wallet owners out of their recovery phrase. On an infected PC, the request comes from inside the wallet's own desktop software. Sometimes it waits until you plug...

item.title: Elevated errors on multiple models
item.description: Jul 15, 15:11 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 15, 15:02 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 15, 14:49 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 15, 14:04 UTC Investigating - We are currently...

item.title: Unpatched Cursor Vulnerability Exposes Users to Code Execution
item.description: An attacker can create a malicious repository containing a git.exe in the project root, and Cursor executes it automatically. The post Unpatched Cursor Vulnerability Exposes Users to Code Execution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: CISA Urges Immediate Patching of Exploited SharePoint Vulnerabilities
item.description: Three vulnerabilities are actively exploited in attacks, including two that have been targeted as zero-days. The post CISA Urges Immediate Patching of Exploited SharePoint Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Firefox, Chrome, Adobe, and VMware Updates Fix Multiple Critical Security Flaws
item.description: Mozilla has released updates to address two critical flaws in Firefox for which it warned that exploit code has been published. The vulnerabilities are listed below - CVE-2026-15718, an invalid pointer in the JavaScript: WebAssembly component CVE-2026-15719, a site isolation in the DOM: Navigation...

item.title: Network Performance issues - Increased HTTP 502 Errors in HKG
item.description: Jul 15, 11:00 UTC Monitoring - Cloudflare is investigating an increased level of HTTP 502 errors originating from the Hong Kong datacenter. We are working to analyse and mitigate this problem. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: Windows Bind Link Attacks Can Hide Malware From EDR Tools
item.description: Bitdefender researchers show how Windows bind links can create conflicting filesystem views to hide malware from endpoint security products. The post Windows Bind Link Attacks Can Hide Malware From EDR Tools appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Virtual Event Today: Cloud & Data Security Summit
item.description: Attendees will be able to interact with leading solution providers and other end users facing similar challenges in securing a variety of cloud deployments. The post Virtual Event Today: Cloud & Data Security Summit appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: a useful quantum machine and a record-breaking subsea tunnel
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. PsiQuantum has a plan to make a massive quantum computer out of light The machine that could change the world will be housed in a room that looks...

item.title: Helping small businesses with free, hands-on cyber consultancy
item.description: Cyber Advisors are offering free 30-minute consultations to help small businesses get started with cyber security.

item.title: SASE Has An AI Blind Spot. Inspecting Packets Is No Longer Enough.
item.description: For years, routing traffic through cloud proxies was good enough. Then work moved to the browser, AI entered the workflow, and the inspection model stopped keeping up. Enterprise workflows now live across SaaS applications, browsers, and an expanding ecosystem of generative AI tools, unsanctioned...

item.title: Researcher Drops New Windows Zero-Day PoC Hours After Microsoft Patch Tuesday
item.description: Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse) has released a new proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit called LegacyHive. It has been described as a Windows User Profile Service arbitrary hive load elevation of privileges vulnerability. The Windows User Profile Service, also referred to as...

item.title: Elevated errors affecting ChatGPT
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Image Generation (Operational) GPTs (Operational) Codex in ChatGPT Desktop (Operational) ChatGPT Work (Operational) Login (Operational) File uploads (Operational) Compliance API (Operational) Deep Research...

item.title: Issue affecting voice mode in ChatGPT
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Voice mode (Operational)

item.title: Introducing Real World VoiceEQ: Measuring the human quality of voice AI
Welcome Inkling by Thinking Machines
[AINews] not much happened today
item.description: a continuation: Codex adding 1M users a day now.

item.title: 5 Trends That Defined AI Engineering at World’s Fair 2026
item.description: At this year's AIE World’s Fair, AI engineering entered a new phase: building systems around agents, rather than just building with agents.

item.title: Partial outage of claude.ai: container creation
item.description: Jul 14, 22:16 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 14, 21:58 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results Jul 14, 21:56 UTC Update - We are continuing to investigate this issue. Jul 14, 21:31 UTC Investigating - We are investigating an outage...

item.title: UPDATE: Google Cloud VMware Engine (GCVE) Stretched Cluster customers are experiencing zonal outages impacting network connectivity across multiple regions.
item.description: Incident began at 2026-07-14 10:00 (all times are US/Pacific).Summary Google Cloud VMware Engine (GCVE) Stretched Cluster customers are experiencing zonal outages impacting network connectivity across multiple regions. Description We are experiencing an issue with Google Cloud VMware Engine (GCVE)...
